job_description:
Senior B2B Content Specialist

2Base Technologies is at a deliberate stage of building its marketing function, and this role owns the written voice of that function end to end.

About 2Base Technologies We are a technology services company with over 15 years of delivery experience, working with mid-market and enterprise clients across the United States and the United Kingdom. Our work spans AI implementation, custom software development, legacy system modernisation, and operational transformation.

Role Overview This is a senior writing role with a specific, demanding purpose. It is not a content management role or a social media role. If that distinction is unclear from the outset, this is probably not the right fit.

What This Role Exists to Do - Operationalise, apply, and enforce the company's written voice across all content. - Target technical leaders and business decision-makers at mid-market companies (CTOs, IT Directors, Founders, COOs, Operations leaders). - Ensure content is technically credible, commercially relevant, and written with a clear point of view.

What You Will Own - Long-form content: pillar pages, blogs, playbooks, decision frameworks, and use-case libraries. - Content written in the voice of senior leadership for professional publishing. - Auditing all published content to meet brand standards. - Writing clear content briefs for junior team members. - Acting as the content quality gate (no content published without approval). - Using AI tools as a productivity layer, not a substitute for thinking.

What You Will Not Own - SEO keyword selection (handled by the SEO Specialist). - Social media scheduling (managed by another team member). - Visual design of content assets (owned by the Visual Designer). - Distribution strategy (decided by the Marketing & Growth Lead). - ICP definition (you write for the ICP, but do not define it).

Requirements - Experience: 3–5 years in B2B content writing (strong candidates have 5–6 years). - Proven content that ranked on Google, not just published. - Ability to write in voices other than your own (executives, founders, senior professionals). - Working-level SEO understanding (search intent, writing to intent). - Experience with AI writing tools (understand their value and limits). - Content that contributed to measurable outcomes (traffic, leads, engagement). - Background in technology, SaaS, or professional services preferred.

Writing Ability - Write with a clear point of view, not neutral summaries. - Explain complex technical concepts in plain language without losing credibility. - Write concisely, avoiding filler and unnecessary qualification. - Adapt to different voices authentically.

Mindset - Treat content as a commercial tool, not a creative exercise. - Comfortable with feedback and editing. - Curious about AI, technology, and business operations. - Fast-paced: deliver first drafts quickly.

What We Are Not Looking For - Portfolios of generic "Top 10 Tips" articles or trend summaries. - Writers who cannot explain their content's performance or impact. - Candidates without experience writing for technical or senior business audiences. - Writers who only produce self-directed content and ignore briefs. - Candidates who cannot write convincingly in voices other than their own. - Candidates who treat AI tools as the primary author with minimal editing.

Benefits - A growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously.

Contact If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: Senior B2B Content Specialist at 2Base Technologies shapes the company's written voice for technical and business audiences. Requires 3-5 years of SEO-driven content creation and AI tool expertise.

job_description:
Organic Growth & SEO Specialist

2Base Technologies is building its marketing function from a clear brief, and this role owns the organic search engine that sits at the centre of it. We are a technology services company with over 15 years of delivery experience, working with mid-market and enterprise clients across the United States and the United Kingdom. Our work spans AI implementation, custom software development, legacy system modernisation, and operational transformation.

What This Role Exists to Do

This role does not optimise content after it is written. It determines what gets written, why, and how it is structured so that the right people find it at the right moment in their decision process. The organic search function at 2Base is being built to generate qualified inbound pipeline from two specific markets, the US and UK, at scale. That means a large volume of high-intent pages, a technical foundation that performs, and a programmatic architecture that compounds over time. The person who fills this role designs that system and owns its results.

What You Will Own

  • The organic traffic strategy for the US and UK markets, including keyword targeting and the full architecture of how search drives inbound pipeline
  • Keyword research and search intent mapping across all service lines and target audiences
  • Page architecture of the website, including what pages exist, what they target, how they are structured, and how they connect through internal linking
  • On-page SEO across all published pages
  • Technical SEO oversight, working directly with the development team on crawlability, indexing, site speed, Core Web Vitals, and structured data
  • Google Search Console as a working instrument, including weekly review, issue resolution, and opportunity identification
  • The programmatic SEO build, including designing the framework and templates that allow the site to scale to 80 to 120+ high-intent pages
  • SEO briefs written clearly enough that the Content Specialist can execute without guessing
  • Link building target approval. The outreach team identifies candidates, and you approve or reject based on defined quality standards
  • SEO performance reporting, including organic traffic, keyword movement, pages published, and Search Console health

What You Will Not Own

  • Writing content: you brief the Content Specialist, you do not write the pages
  • Link building outreach: a dedicated team member executes this, you set the standard
  • Paid search: that is a separate role
  • Social media distribution: separate channel, separate owner
  • Content calendar scheduling: the Marketing & Growth Lead sets distribution priorities

Requirements

Experience - Minimum 3 to 5 years in an SEO or organic growth role, with strong candidates typically at the 4 to 6 year mark - Experience ranking high-intent commercial pages, not just informational content - Experience managing technical SEO independently - Experience building or contributing to a programmatic SEO architecture - Strong working knowledge of Google Search Console as an active tool - Experience working across US and/or UK markets - Working knowledge of AEO, GEO, and AIO, including AI-powered search surfaces - Ability to write SEO briefs that a content writer can execute without clarification

Mindset - Thinks in systems, not tasks - Comfortable with ambiguity in early-stage environments - Analytically honest - Collaborative but not passive

What We Are Not Looking For

  • SEO practitioners whose entire experience is informational content
  • Candidates who treat Search Console as a monthly reporting task
  • Candidates who cannot explain high-intent vs. informational keywords
  • Candidates whose SEO process depends entirely on tools
  • Candidates with no exposure to US or UK search behaviour

Benefits

  • A growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously.

If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: Strategic SEO role focused on building a scalable organic growth system for US/UK markets, owning keyword strategy, technical architecture, and performance-driven content frameworks.

job_description:
About the Role

We are seeking a detail-oriented Associate QA Engineer with 1–3 years of hands-on experience in software testing to join our QA team. In this role, you will ensure the quality and reliability of web and mobile applications through test planning, test case creation, test execution, and defect tracking.

Key Responsibilities

  • Review BRD, SRS, user stories, and functional specifications to understand system requirements.
  • Translate requirements into structured test scenarios and documented test cases for comprehensive coverage.
  • Perform functional, regression, and exploratory testing across web and mobile platforms.
  • Identify edge cases, alternate flows, and boundary conditions to validate system behavior.
  • Log, document, and track defects with clear reproduction steps and severity classification.
  • Validate bug fixes and conduct regression testing to maintain stability during releases.
  • Maintain test documentation, execution reports, and QA artifacts for traceability.
  • Support test summaries, progress updates, and QA reports during development cycles.
  • Collaborate with business analysts, developers, and senior QA engineers to clarify requirements and resolve issues.
  • Participate in Agile/Scrum ceremonies (sprint planning, stand-ups, reviews).
  • Perform basic API validation and data verification for integration-level testing.
  • Own assigned modules/features to ensure testing completeness and release readiness.
  • Contribute to improving test coverage, testing approaches, and QA processes.
  • Validate AI-powered/data-driven features for output accuracy and system behavior.
  • Learn automation fundamentals and emerging QA tools, including AI testing practices.

Required Skills & Mindset

  • Strong attention to detail and structured thinking.
  • Willingness to deeply understand business logic before testing.
  • Ability to identify edge cases and potential product issues.
  • Analytical mindset to question unexpected system behavior.
  • Clear written/verbal communication for collaboration.
  • Strong documentation skills for defect reporting and test case writing.
  • Ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment.
  • Solid understanding of functional, regression, integration, and exploratory testing.
  • Experience with test case design techniques.
  • Familiarity with bug tracking and test management tools.
  • Basic exposure to API testing (Postman, REST Assured).
  • Understanding of Agile/Scrum methodologies and QA’s role in sprints.
  • Familiarity with AI-assisted testing.

Good to Have

  • Basic awareness of performance and security testing.
  • Interest in learning automation frameworks (Selenium, Playwright, Cypress).
  • Scripting knowledge in Python, JavaScript, or Java.
  • Exposure to AI-enabled applications (chatbots, recommendation engines).
  • Awareness of modern QA practices like continuous testing.
  • Ability to use AI-assisted tools for test case generation and data preparation.
  • Experience with mobile testing frameworks (Appium).
  • Risk-based testing and prioritization mindset.
  • Commitment to continuous learning in QA tools/methodologies.
  • ISTQB certification or equivalent.

Experience & Education

  • 1–3 years of experience.
  • B.Tech in IT/CS or MCA.

Contact: Share your resume at careers@2basetechnologies.com.

job_description:
Sr. Business Analyst

At 2Base, we are rebuilding the Business Analyst function into a world-class, product-minded, clarity-driven team. This role is not a documentation role. It is not a screen updater role. It is not a support role. This is a core Business Analyst position responsible for functional modelling, system reasoning, requirement clarity, and building the foundation for correct development.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead requirement workshops, client calls, and discovery discussions to understand business needs clearly.
  • Ask deeper questions, challenge assumptions, and validate the true business intent behind every requirement.
  • Convert ambiguous client inputs into structured, logical, and actionable functional understanding.
  • Break large features into modules, workflows, components, and clear dependencies.
  • Build strong system-level understanding involving APIs, integrations, data flow, and backend considerations.
  • Translate business processes into functional logic that development teams can execute.
  • Decompose requirements into use-case flows, system behaviours, edge cases, alternate paths, validation rules, and dependencies.
  • Prepare high-quality documentation, including BRD, FRD, SRS, workflows, state diagrams, data-flow diagrams, API requirement specs, and acceptance criteria.
  • Ensure all documentation is clear, logical, structured, testable, and ready for developers and QA to use.
  • Collaborate closely with developers, architects, QA, and product owners to validate feasibility and align understanding.
  • Ensure QA teams can derive test cases effectively from your functional logic and acceptance criteria.
  • Communicate confidently with clients, handle requirement discussions smoothly, and clarify misunderstandings immediately.
  • Push back politely on unclear or changing requirements and ensure final decisions are documented properly.
  • Maintain requirement traceability throughout the lifecycle and ensure zero ambiguity during handover to development.
  • Own the entire requirement lifecycle from discovery to development readiness with high accountability.
  • Drive clarity across teams by ensuring every requirement is complete, validated, and logically consistent.

Required Skills & Experience

  • Minimum 4+ years of core Business Analyst experience with strong analytical and system-level thinking.
  • Ability to break down complex requirements into clear components, workflows, and functional logic.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication with confidence in client interactions and clarifications.
  • Proven experience in preparing SRS, BRD, FRD, workflows, and acceptance criteria with high clarity.
  • Ability to challenge assumptions politely, ask the right questions, and ensure zero ambiguity.
  • Strong collaboration experience with development, QA, architects, and cross-functional teams.
  • Understanding of API behaviours, integrations, data flows, and multi-module system interactions.
  • Ability to run requirement discussions, workshops, and independently lead discovery sessions.
  • Exposure to SaaS, scalable products, automation, or AI-supported systems is an added advantage.
